LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED BECAUSE THERE IS TRIABLE ISSUE AS TO WHETHER MR. WATKINS EXPRESSED AN INTEREST IN THAT PROMOTION AND THE CITY HAS FAILED TO PRESENT EVIDENCE SATISFYING IT'S INITIAL BURDEN OF PRODUCTION AS TO EITHER THE ABSENCE OF A PRIMA FACIE CASE OR LEGITIMATE OR NON-LEGITIMATE REASON FOR MR. WATKINS NOT RECEIVING THAT PROMOTION. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CCP 437C(G) AND CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR	711, CKAREN@SFTC.ORG

Defendant City And County Of San Francisco'S Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Jun-20-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Gregory Bock, Esq. (510-841-1171) of Brock Law Office for Plaintiff.
Amy Super, Deputy City Attorney (415-554-3931) and Kenneth Walczak, (415-554-4206) Deputy City Attorney for Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court asks Plaintiff to identify adverse employment actions and evidence of a causal link beween adverse employment action and protected activity. The Court denies Plaintiff's objections to untimely hearing. The Court rules as follows: Defendant City And County Of San Francisco'S Motion For Summary Judgment is Denied because there is triable issue as to whether Mr. Watkins expressed an interest in that promotion and the City has failed to present evidence satisfying it's initial burden of production as to either the absence of a Prima Facie case or legitimate or non-legitimate reason for Mr. Watkins not receiving that promotion. Prevailing party to submit order in compliance with CCP 437c(g) and CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR 711, ckaren@sftc.org =(302/HK)
